headline: 33% of target responses collected; strong regional spread but scale readiness concerns.
collection_status: 458 of 1,350 responses (34%) secured across all 10 regions. Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 47% of current data. Collection pace supports on-time completion if momentum holds; mid-survey acceleration needed to close 892-response gap by deadline.
key_findings: KiuFunza expansion dominates priorities (36% of responses), with 165 stakeholders backing expansion over strengthening existing work (73 responses).
Teacher engagement strong: 248 KiuFunza teachers + 109 head teachers represent 78% of respondents; volunteer and WEO participation balanced at 34 each.
Satisfaction-readiness gap flagged: 4.4/5 partner satisfaction contrasts sharply with 2.8/5 scale readiness, suggesting enthusiasm without operational confidence.
Feedback reveals three actionable demands: sustained teacher incentives, digital monitoring systems, and policy integration—not just programme expansion.
flags: Scale readiness 2.8/5 indicates stakeholders lack confidence in scaling infrastructure; risk of expansion commitments without institutional foundation.
Underrepresentation of district/regional officials (DEO, REO, ARC, REC = 24 responses): essential policy voices under-captured; skews sample toward school-level perspectives.
Lindi-Mara concentration (167/458, 36%): uneven regional distribution may mask Rukwa (15), Singida (25), Songwe (29) specific barriers; validate coverage balance.
recommendation: Immediately prioritise targeted outreach to DEO, REO, and policy-level officials in undersampled regions (Rukwa, Singida, Songwe) to rebalance sample and test whether scale readiness gap is perception or structural. Simultaneously, use feedback themes (incentives, digital systems, policy alignment) to stress-test expansion strategy against operational readiness findings.
